title: Introduction
content:
## Introduction

The Core Information Standard Version 2 (CISV2) defines the essential information elements required for effective health and care delivery across the NHS and social care settings. This standard ensures consistent capture and sharing of person-centred information to support integrated care.

### Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides implementation guidance for CISV2, enabling system developers and healthcare organisations to adopt the standard effectively. The scope encompasses demographic details, clinical information, care planning, legal frameworks, and social care elements required for comprehensive person-centred records.

### Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder consultation, CISV2 builds upon established NHS data standards while incorporating person-centred care principles. The standard addresses the need for interoperable records that support both clinical decision-making and person-directed care planning across health and social care boundaries.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Health Interventions & Recommendations

CISV2 supports comprehensive recording of health interventions across care settings. Key recommendations focus on ensuring complete, accurate, and accessible information capture.

### Key Recommendations

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person Demographics | Capture NHS number with verification status for all records | NHS Data Dictionary | Mandatory |
| Reasonable Adjustments | Record all adjustments per Equality Act 2010 requirements | Accessible Information Standard | Required |
| Medications Management | Use dm+d coding for all medication entries | NICE Medicines Optimisation | Required |
| Care Planning | Document goals, actions and outcomes with person agreement | NICE Guidelines NG86 | Recommended |
| Safeguarding | Flag all safeguarding concerns with clear escalation pathways | NHS Safeguarding Guidelines | Mandatory |
| Mental Capacity | Record capacity assessments for relevant decisions | Mental Capacity Act 2005 | Required |
| End of Life Care | Document CPR decisions and preferred place of care/death | NHS End of Life Care | Required |

### Implementation Context

These interventions support integrated care delivery by ensuring critical information is available at the point of care. The standard mandates structured data capture using established terminologies (SNOMED CT, dm+d) while allowing free text for person-specific details.

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Generic Personas

The following personas represent key users and stakeholders of the Core Information Standard.

| Persona ID | Title | Description | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | General Practitioner | Primary care physician managing ongoing patient care | Clinical assessment, care coordination, medication management, SNOMED CT coding |
| CISV2.P2 | Social Worker | Professional coordinating social care services and safeguarding | Needs assessment, safeguarding procedures, care planning, multi-agency working |
| CISV2.P3 | Community Nurse | Nurse providing care in home/community settings | Clinical procedures, medication administration, care planning, wound management |
| CISV2.P4 | Care Home Manager | Manager overseeing residential care provision | Care quality oversight, regulatory compliance, staff management, care planning |
| CISV2.P5 | Hospital Consultant | Specialist physician providing acute/specialist care | Specialist diagnostics, treatment planning, multidisciplinary coordination |
| CISV2.P6 | Person/Patient | Individual receiving health and social care services | Self-advocacy, care preferences, consent provision, care plan participation |
| CISV2.P7 | Family Carer | Family member providing informal care and support | Personal care, medication support, advocacy, care coordination |
| CISV2.P8 | Information Manager | Professional managing health/care information systems | Data quality, system integration, information governance, technical standards |

### Persona Context

These personas interact with CISV2 data throughout the care journey. The standard must support their diverse needs while maintaining person-centred principles and ensuring appropriate access controls based on role and consent.

title: User Scenarios
content:
## User Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Emergency Hospital Admission with Reasonable Adjustments

Mrs Chen, who has autism and communication needs, is brought to A&E following a fall at home. The triage nurse accesses her CISV2 record, immediately seeing flagged reasonable adjustments including her need for written communication and a quiet environment.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | Triage Nurse (CISV2.P3), Mrs Chen (CISV2.P6), A&E Consultant (CISV2.P5) |
| Trigger | Emergency admission requiring immediate access to adjustment needs |
| Data Elements | Reasonable adjustments, communication preferences, medical history, medications |
| Outcome | Appropriate adjustments made, reducing distress and improving care quality |

### Scenario 2: Integrated Care Planning

Mr Patel is being discharged from hospital following a stroke. The multidisciplinary team uses CISV2 to coordinate his care plan including physiotherapy, speech therapy, and social care support.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | Hospital Consultant (CISV2.P5), Social Worker (CISV2.P2), Community Nurse (CISV2.P3), Mr Patel (CISV2.P6) |
| Trigger | Hospital discharge requiring coordinated care planning |
| Data Elements | Care plan goals, professional contacts, service provision, equipment needs |
| Outcome | Seamless transition with all services aligned to support recovery |

### Scenario 3: Safeguarding Alert Management

A care home manager identifies potential financial abuse of a resident with dementia. They record the concern in CISV2, triggering appropriate safeguarding procedures.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | Care Home Manager (CISV2.P4), Social Worker (CISV2.P2), Resident (CISV2.P6) |
| Trigger | Identification of potential safeguarding issue |
| Data Elements | Safeguarding flags, mental capacity assessment, incident details, action taken |
| Outcome | Timely safeguarding response with full audit trail maintained |

title: References
content:
## References

### Standards and Specifications

1. NHS Data Dictionary. Available at: https://datadictionary.nhs.uk
2. SNOMED CT UK Edition. NHS Digital. Available at: https://digital.nhs.uk/services/terminology-and-classifications/snomed-ct
3. Dictionary of Medicines and Devices (dm+d). NHS Business Services Authority. Available at: https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd
4. FHIR UK Core Implementation Guide. NHS Digital. Available at: https://simplifier.net/hl7fhirukcorer4

### Legislation and Policy

5. Equality Act 2010. UK Government. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents
6. Mental Capacity Act 2005. UK Government. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/9/contents
7. NHS Accessible Information Standard. NHS England. Available at: https://www.england.nhs.uk/about/equality/equality-hub/patient-equalities-programme/equality-frameworks-and-information-standards/accessibleinfo/

### Clinical Guidelines

8. NICE NG86: People's experience in adult social care services. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence. 2018
9. NICE NG197: Shared decision making. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence. 2021
10. NHS Safeguarding Accountability and Assurance Framework. NHS England. 2022

### Technical Resources

11. PRSB Standards for the Structure and Content of Health and Care Records. Professional Record Standards Body. Available at: https://theprsb.org/standards/
12. UCUM: The Unified Code for Units of Measure. Available at: https://ucum.org
13. HL7 FHIR R4 Specification. Health Level Seven International. Available at: https://www.hl7.org/fhir/

title: Business Processes & Workflows
content: The Core Information Standard defines five key business processes that support integrated care delivery. These range from initial person registration and assessment through to ongoing care coordination and information sharing. Each process involves multiple personas working collaboratively to ensure comprehensive, person-centred information management.

processes_data.summary_markdown: The Core Information Standard defines five key business processes that support integrated care delivery. These range from initial person registration and assessment through to ongoing care coordination and information sharing. Each process involves multiple personas working collaboratively to ensure comprehensive, person-centred information management.

activities.title: Person Registration and Demographics Management
activities.objectives: Establish accurate person identity and demographic information to enable safe care delivery and information sharing across services

activities.title: Initial Assessment and Care Planning
activities.personas: Social Worker, Community Nurse, Person/Patient, Family Carer
activities.objectives: Conduct comprehensive assessment of person's needs, preferences and circumstances to develop personalised care plan

activities.title: Medication Management Process
activities.personas: General Practitioner, Community Nurse, Hospital Consultant
activities.objectives: Ensure safe prescribing, supply and administration of medications with appropriate monitoring and review

activities.title: Care Transition and Discharge
activities.personas: Hospital Consultant, Social Worker, Community Nurse, Care Home Manager
activities.objectives: Coordinate safe transition between care settings with comprehensive handover of information

activities.title: Information Sharing for Integrated Care
activities.personas: Information Manager, General Practitioner, Social Worker, Person/Patient
activities.objectives: Enable secure, consent-based sharing of information across health and social care boundaries

title: Core Data Elements
content:
## Core Data Elements

The Core Information Standard defines essential data elements organised by functional domains to support comprehensive person-centred records.

### Person Demographics

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE.01 | Person Name | Full name components including first, family, preferred names | String | M | NHS DD: Person name |
| CISV2.DE.02 | NHS Number | Unique NHS identifier with verification status | String[10] | M | NHS DD: NHS number |
| CISV2.DE.03 | Date of Birth | Person's date of birth | Date | M | NHS DD: Person birth date |
| CISV2.DE.04 | Gender | Person's stated gender | String[1] | R | NHS DD: Person stated gender |
| CISV2.DE.05 | Contact Details | Phone, email, preferred contact method | Complex | R | NHS DD: Contact details |
| CISV2.DE.06 | GP Practice | Registered GP practice identifier | String[5] | M | NHS DD: GP practice code |

### Individual Requirements

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE.07 | Reasonable Adjustments | Adjustments required under Equality Act | Boolean + Text | R | SNOMED: 1108111000000107 |
| CISV2.DE.08 | Communication Needs | Specific communication requirements | Complex | R | SNOMED: AIS refsets |
| CISV2.DE.09 | Impairments | Recorded impairments affecting care | Complex | R | SNOMED CT |
| CISV2.DE.10 | Mobility Needs | Mobility requirements and aids | Complex | R | SNOMED: 999002551000000108 |

### Clinical Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE.11 | Problem List | Active problems requiring management | Complex | M | SNOMED: 1127581000000103 |
| CISV2.DE.12 | Medications | Current medications with dm+d coding | Complex | M | dm+d codes |
| CISV2.DE.13 | Allergies | Allergies and adverse reactions | Complex | M | SNOMED/dm+d |
| CISV2.DE.14 | Vaccinations | Immunisation records | Complex | R | dm+d/SNOMED |

### Legal and Safeguarding

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE.15 | Mental Capacity | Mental capacity assessment records | Complex | R | Free text |
| CISV2.DE.16 | Safeguarding Flags | Active safeguarding concerns | Boolean + Detail | R | SNOMED: 999002381000000108 |
| CISV2.DE.17 | LPA Status | Lasting Power of Attorney records | Complex | R | SNOMED: 999001951000000107 |
| CISV2.DE.18 | DNACPR Decision | CPR decision and documentation | Complex | R | SNOMED: 450475007/450476008 |

### Care Planning

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE.19 | Care Goals | Person's goals and preferences | Complex | R | SNOMED context codes |
| CISV2.DE.20 | Care Actions | Planned actions with responsibilities | Complex | R | SNOMED procedures |
| CISV2.DE.21 | Review Dates | Scheduled review dates | DateTime | R | Free text |
| CISV2.DE.22 | Contingency Plans | Emergency/deterioration plans | Complex | R | Free text |

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Decision-Support Logic

Decision tables support consistent application of business rules and clinical guidelines within the Core Information Standard.

### Table 1: Reasonable Adjustment Requirements

**Description:** Determines required adjustments based on person's impairments and needs

| Condition | Required Action |
|---|---|
| Hearing impairment + Prefers BSL | Provide BSL interpreter for appointments |
| Visual impairment + Uses braille | Provide information in braille format |
| Autism spectrum + Sensory sensitivity | Offer quiet environment, minimise waiting |
| Learning disability + Needs support | Allow extra time, provide easy-read materials |
| Mobility impairment + Uses wheelchair | Ensure wheelchair access, adjustable furniture |
| Communication difficulty + Uses AAC | Support use of communication aids |

### Table 2: Safeguarding Escalation

**Description:** Guides safeguarding response based on identified concerns

| Risk Indicators | Immediate Actions |
|---|---|
| Physical abuse indicators | Record details, notify safeguarding lead within 2 hours |
| Financial exploitation suspected | Secure finances if possible, alert social services same day |
| Neglect with immediate harm risk | Ensure safety, contact emergency services if needed |
| Self-neglect affecting health | Assess capacity, involve mental health team |
| Domestic abuse disclosed | Provide safety information, MARAC referral if high risk |

### Table 3: Medication Review Triggers

**Description:** Identifies when medication reviews are required

| Condition | Review Requirement |
|---|---|
| Age ≥75 + ≥4 regular medications | Annual review minimum |
| New diagnosis affecting medication | Review within 1 month |
| Hospital admission in last 3 months | Review within 2 weeks of discharge |
| Falls or confusion reported | Urgent review within 48 hours |
| Renal function decline >20% | Review within 1 week |
| Adherence concerns identified | Review at next contact |

### Table 4: Mental Capacity Assessment Triggers

**Description:** Determines when formal capacity assessment needed

| Scenario | Assessment Requirement |
|---|---|
| Refusing essential treatment | Assess capacity for specific decision |
| Major care transition proposed | Assess capacity for residence decision |
| Financial abuse concerns | Assess capacity for financial decisions |
| Fluctuating confusion reported | Time-specific assessments needed |
| Family disagreement on care | Formal assessment and best interests process |

### Table 5: Information Sharing Decisions

**Description:** Guides lawful information sharing decisions

| Request Context | Sharing Decision |
|---|---|
| Direct care + professional request | Share with explicit consent or implied consent for direct care |
| Safeguarding concern + statutory request | Share under legal duty, inform person unless increases risk |
| Research request + identifiable data | Requires explicit consent or CAG approval |
| Family request + person has capacity | Only with person's consent |
| Emergency treatment needed | Share minimum necessary for immediate care |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Indicators & Performance Metrics

Key indicators monitor implementation quality and outcomes of the Core Information Standard.

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.01 | NHS Number Verification Rate | Persons with verified NHS number | All registered persons | By organisation, age group | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.02 | Reasonable Adjustment Recording | Persons with disabilities having adjustments recorded | Persons identified with disabilities | By disability type, organisation | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.03 | Consent Documentation Completeness | Persons with valid consent records | All persons with shared records | By consent type, care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.04 | Care Plan Currency | Persons with care plan reviewed in last 12 months | All persons with active care plans | By service type, complexity | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.05 | Medication Reconciliation Timeliness | Medication reviews completed within 48h of admission | All emergency admissions on medications | By ward, admission type | Weekly |
| CISV2.IND.06 | Safeguarding Response Time | Safeguarding alerts actioned within required timeframe | All safeguarding alerts raised | By risk level, organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.07 | Person-Reported Outcome Recording | Persons with goals/preferences documented | All persons with care plans | By service, age group | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.08 | Information Quality Score | Records meeting all mandatory field requirements | All active person records | By data domain, organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.09 | Integrated Care Coordination | Persons with documented MDT involvement | Persons with complex needs (2+ LTCs) | By locality, care model | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.10 | Digital Inclusion Support | Persons offered digital access support | Persons requiring reasonable adjustments | By adjustment type | Annual |

### Implementation Notes

- Indicators should be calculated from routine data capture, not requiring additional collection
- Disaggregation supports equality monitoring and service improvement
- Thresholds for achievement should be locally agreed based on baseline assessment
- Regular review ensures indicators remain relevant to person-centred outcomes

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR.01 | Person Identity Management | System must support NHS number verification and demographic matching across multiple identifiers | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.02 | Consent Management | Capture, store and enforce consent decisions for information sharing with full audit trail | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.03 | Reasonable Adjustment Alerts | Display adjustment requirements prominently at point of care with acknowledgement tracking | High |
| CISV2.FR.04 | Integrated Care Planning | Support collaborative care planning with multiple professional inputs and person agreement | High |
| CISV2.FR.05 | Medication Reconciliation | Enable medication list compilation from multiple sources with conflict resolution | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.06 | Safeguarding Workflows | Trigger alerts and workflows based on safeguarding indicators with escalation tracking | High |
| CISV2.FR.07 | Document Management | Store and retrieve clinical documents with appropriate metadata and access controls | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.08 | Appointment Scheduling | Book appointments considering reasonable adjustments and preferences | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.09 | Information Exchange | Support standards-based information exchange using FHIR UK Core | High |
| CISV2.FR.10 | Reporting and Analytics | Generate required performance indicators and statutory reports | Medium |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR.01 | Performance | Page load <2 seconds, search results <3 seconds for 95% of requests | High |
| CISV2.NFR.02 | Availability | 99.5% uptime during core hours (8am-8pm), 24/7 for critical functions | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.03 | Security | NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit compliance, encryption at rest and in transit | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.04 | Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, support for assistive technologies | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.05 | Scalability | Support 100,000+ active records with linear performance scaling | High |
| CISV2.NFR.06 | Interoperability | HL7 FHIR R4 compliance, NHS Integration standards | High |
| CISV2.NFR.07 | Auditability | Complete audit trail for all data access and changes, 7-year retention | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.08 | Usability | Task completion rate >90% without training for core functions | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.09 | Mobile Support | Responsive design supporting tablets and smartphones for community use | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.10 | Disaster Recovery | RPO <1 hour, RTO <4 hours for critical functions | High |
